title: C++ Source
documentation:
## Purpose
C++ source files contain implementation code written in C++, a systems-level language that extends C with object-oriented programming, templates, and the standard library. C++ is used for game engines, real-time systems, compilers, high-performance libraries, and any domain where low-level control and high performance are both required.

## When to use
Use [C++ Source](https://capdag.com/browse/media:cpp;textable;code) for implementation files with extensions `.cpp`, `.cc`, `.cxx`, or `.c++`. Header declarations belong in [C++ Header](https://capdag.com/browse/media:cpp-header;textable;code). For files mixing C++ with Objective-C, see [Objective-C++ Source](https://capdag.com/browse/media:objcpp;textable;code).

## Notes
C++ standard versions (C++11, 14, 17, 20, 23) introduce significant language changes; tools must know the applicable standard to parse source correctly. Template-heavy code and metaprogramming patterns can be challenging for static analysis tools. Build systems (CMake, Meson, Bazel) control compilation flags and the C++ standard version used.
